title: A dream
wikitext:
[]: "A dream?"             Quote Post by Zuggy » Mon Jul 03, 2006 2:46 pm What does Rei mean when she speaks those words? If you have no dreams that entails to me that your not ensouled/you lack electrical coherence, or is there a psychological explanation for lack of dreaming/recollection of dreams?

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Jul 03, 2006 2:57 pm Some animals dream. Does that mean that animals have souls? Does this mean Pen Pen complemented? What would his complementation be like?

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Mon Jul 03, 2006 3:07 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: Some animals dream. Does that mean that animals have souls?

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Jul 03, 2006 3:38 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: Does this mean Pen Pen complemented? What would his complementation be like?

[]:             Quote Post by ZeroFive1 » Mon Jul 03, 2006 4:00 pm Mr. Tines wrote: Misato's curry sauce.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Jul 03, 2006 4:56 pm ZeroFive1 wrote: How can Shinji live like that???

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Tue Jul 04, 2006 9:37 am Mr. Tines wrote: As Asuka is six months younger than Shinji , and present in Complementation, I think we can safely say that Shinji is not the last ensouled human.

[]:             Quote Post by ZeroFive1 » Tue Jul 04, 2006 9:55 am No, I checked. Asuka was born in december, Shinji was born in june.

[]:             Quote Post by bp32 » Tue Jul 04, 2006 10:25 am ZeroFive1 wrote: No, I checked. Asuka was born in december, Shinji was born in june.

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Tue Jul 04, 2006 10:45 am Shinji can't be the last insouled human toji has a little sister remember

[]:             Quote Post by ZeroFive1 » Tue Jul 04, 2006 11:34 am DatDude wrote: Shinji can't be the last insouled human toji has a little sister remember

[]:             Quote Post by bp32 » Tue Jul 04, 2006 11:49 am ZeroFive1 wrote: Also, I checked the wiki. I'm not sure if it's that reliable, though.

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Tue Jul 04, 2006 12:28 pm ZeroFive1 wrote: No, I checked. Asuka was born in december, Shinji was born in june.

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Tue Jul 04, 2006 12:40 pm ZeroFive1 wrote: Zugzwang wrote: Yes. Anything that is alive has a soul, humans have the ability to self-steer their soul's path with conscious intent whereas animals don't...

[]:             Quote Post by ZeroFive1 » Tue Jul 04, 2006 12:51 pm I was under the assumption that the 14 year olds were the last humans with souls.

[]:             Quote Post by bp32 » Tue Jul 04, 2006 12:55 pm ZeroFive1 wrote: I was under the assumption that the 14 year olds were the last humans with sould.

[]:             Quote Post by Faußtin » Tue Jul 04, 2006 2:22 pm He-he-hey, I guess we are in trouble with the word "soul". Lets define it; that should make it a lot easier. I agree with Shin-seiki; all who/what lives has a soul, though the maturity is another thing. So I guess it is a quite dumb idea that Shinji's generation would have been the last humans with a soul. Or, perhaps, it could be interpreted as "the last living humans", but it is, again, dumb.. Back to the topic. I have no idea of the first question, due to the sad fact that I don't remember a single word from that scene, though the picture is still in my mind. And the music too. Even now, our allmighty scientists have little knowledge about dreams and their mechanism; though there are useful non-approved (non-official) theories. There is difference between "I didn't have a dream" and "I don't remember my dream", a pretty big one to be honest. So if you don't remember it, it doesn't necessarily mean that you didn't have one. This is also effected by your body: if you are very tired, don't expect to remember your dream. But I shouldn't babble about this; I barely dream, or, more accurately, barely remember them. Though sometimes I remember the emotion left behind it, even if I have no idea of my dream. But this happens quite rarely.

[]:             Quote Post by bp32 » Tue Jul 04, 2006 2:26 pm Rei has Lilith's soul, much like Kaworu has Adam's, so it certainly doesn't imply she lacks a soul.

[]:             Quote Post by ZeroFive1 » Tue Jul 04, 2006 2:37 pm The only reason (I belive) that Rei dosen't act normally is because she's confused because she has two souls in her body, or at least copies of the souls (Yui & Lilith).

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue Jul 04, 2006 2:37 pm Shin-seiki wrote: I myself don't take the reported birthdays (which happen to be, for convenience sake, concurrent with the birthday of the characters' seiyuu) very seriously,
